title: Dense Hearty Japanese Buckwheat Jjigae
description: A funky, crumbly, and hearty Japanese meal-prep dish originating from the Tohoku region, using mixed grain, rest-meat, and halal-friendly ingredients. Best served in a cazuela or Dutch oven, with a tendon-like texture.

ingredients.name: Soba-Buckwheat Mix
ingredients.notes: Available at Asian grocery stores or online.

ingredients.name: Diced Pork Belly
ingredients.notes: Use a rest-meat or slow-cooked pork belly for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Kimchi
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, any type of kimchi works well.

ingredients.name: Miso Paste
ingredients.notes: White or red miso paste, whichever you prefer.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium or tamari for a gluten-free option.

ingredients.name: Gochujang
ingredients.notes: Korean chili paste adds depth and heat.

ingredients.name: Brown Sugar
ingredients.notes: Balances the savory and spicy flavors.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate or slice before adding to the dish.

ingredients.name: Korean Chili Flakes
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste, for added heat.

ingredients.name: Toasted Sesame Oil
ingredients.notes: Finishing oil for added nutty flavor.

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of toasted sesame oil in a large cazuela or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add 1-inch piece of grated ginger and 2 cloves of minced garlic, sauté until fragrant.
Add 1 cup of soba-buckwheat mix and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add 1/2 cup of diced pork belly and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as needed.
Add 1/2 cup of kimchi, 2 tablespoons of miso paste, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 1 teaspoon of gochujang, and 1 teaspoon of brown sugar, stirring to combine.
Add 1/4 teaspoon of Korean chili flakes and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve hot, garnished with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions, if desired.
